assumptions: Census median new SF home price $420,000 (2024); NAHB lot share ~18.5%, scaled to $80k
quote_excerpt: Finished lot cost represents 18.5% of sale price
notes: NAHB survey of builders; national average. One-time cost at purchase; no discounting.

formula: Estimated A/E fee for production-home plan adaptation
assumptions: Production homes use stock plans with modest site-specific engineering; $8k covers plan license + structural/MEP engineering
quote_excerpt: Architecture, engineering and related soft costs
notes: Varies widely by market and custom vs. production; estimate based on NAHB soft cost data.

formula: Permit fees for 2,200 ft2 SF residence
assumptions: National average building permit fee for new SF construction
notes: Highly variable by jurisdiction. One-time pre-construction cost.

formula: Estimated impact/development fees for typical SF lot
assumptions: Includes transportation, school, park impact fees where applicable; national average estimate
quote_excerpt: Average impact fee per single-family lot
notes: Many jurisdictions charge no impact fees; others charge $10,000+. $3,500 is blended national estimate.

assumptions: Covers water tap, sewer connection, electric service drop, gas meter set
notes: Varies by utility provider and municipality.

assumptions: Sum of P002-P005
quote_excerpt: Total soft costs for design, permitting, and fees
notes: One-time pre-construction costs. No discounting applied.

formula: Slab-on-grade foundation for 2,200 ft2 footprint (1,100 ft2 slab for 2-story + garage)
assumptions: Includes excavation, grading, formwork, rebar, concrete pour, waterproofing for slab-on-grade
quote_excerpt: Foundation costs including excavation and concrete
notes: Slab-on-grade is least expensive foundation type. ~12% of hard cost.

assumptions: Includes lumber, trusses/rafters, sheathing, labor for walls, floors, roof structure
quote_excerpt: Framing labor and materials represent the largest single trade cost
notes: ~22% of hard cost. Lumber price volatility affects this significantly.

formula: Roofing + siding + windows + exterior doors + insulation
assumptions: Asphalt shingle roof, vinyl/fiber cement siding, vinyl windows, standard entry doors, code-minimum insulation
quote_excerpt: Exterior finishes including roofing, siding, and windows
notes: ~16% of hard cost.

formula: Mechanical + Electrical + Plumbing rough-in and trim-out
assumptions: Includes HVAC system install, electrical wiring/panel/fixtures, plumbing rough/fixtures, gas piping
notes: ~28% of hard cost. Largest combined trade category.

formula: Drywall + paint + flooring + cabinets + countertops + trim + appliances
assumptions: Builder-grade finishes: drywall, paint, LVP/carpet flooring, laminate counters, stock cabinets, builder-pack appliances
notes: ~22% of hard cost.

assumptions: Sum of P007-P011. Includes site work. Slightly below NAHB survey average because median home is smaller/simpler than survey average.
quote_excerpt: Average construction cost for a new single-family home
notes: One-time construction cost. No discounting.

formula: Builder overhead / general conditions for project management, insurance, office, supervision
assumptions: ~6.4% of sale price. Covers project management, builder's risk insurance, temporary utilities, site office, supervision
quote_excerpt: General overhead expenses of the builder
notes: NAHB reports builder overhead separately from profit.

formula: Builder profit margin on $420,000 sale price
assumptions: ~7.6% of sale price. NAHB reports average builder profit margin of 7-9%
quote_excerpt: Builder profit averages approximately 7-9% of sale price
notes: Profit margin varies significantly by market and builder size.

assumptions: Sum of P013 + P014. ~14% of sale price total.
notes: One-time cost at construction. No discounting.

formula: Interest carry on construction loan during ~8-10 month build period
assumptions: Construction loan ~$300k, 8% rate, ~8 month build, 50% average draw = $300k x 0.50 x 0.08 x 0.67yr = $8k, plus fees/points ~ $13k total
quote_excerpt: Construction loan interest and fees during build period
notes: One-time cost incurred during construction. No discounting applied (occurs at year 0).

assumptions: 80% LTV on $420,000 purchase; 30-year fixed at 6.75% (near prevailing rate 2024). Nominal total over 30 years.
source_title: Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Market Survey
notes: PV of mortgage interest at 3% real discount rate: ~$176,722. Annual mortgage payment ~$26,159; PV of payment stream = $26,159 x 19.6004 = $512,722; PV interest = $512,722 - $336,000 = $176,722.

assumptions: Loan origination fee / points paid at closing
quote_excerpt: Origination fees typically range from 0.5% to 1% of loan amount
notes: One-time cost at purchase. No discounting.

assumptions: Sum of P016 + P017 + P018
quote_excerpt: Total financing costs over ownership period
notes: PV at 3% real: ~$192,222. Construction loan + origination are year-0 costs ($15,500); mortgage interest PV = $176,722.

formula: ~2.4% of $420,000 purchase price (excluding origination fee counted in financing)
assumptions: Title insurance, escrow, recording, transfer tax, attorney fees at purchase
quote_excerpt: Average closing costs excluding taxes approximately 2-3%
notes: One-time cost at year 0. No discounting.

formula: ~6% agent commission + closing costs at sale in year 30. Assumes sale near original price in real terms.
assumptions: 6% total real estate commission plus seller closing costs. Home value assumed constant in real (inflation-adjusted) terms for simplicity.
quote_excerpt: Typical total commission of 5-6% of sale price

assumptions: Sum of P020 + P021
quote_excerpt: Total transaction costs over ownership period

assumptions: 1.1% effective property tax rate (national average); assessed value assumed constant in real terms at $420,000
locator: Effective Property Tax Rate by State
quote_excerpt: National average effective property tax rate approximately 1.1%
notes: PV at 3% real: $4,620 x 19.6004 = $90,554. Highly variable by state/county.

assumptions: Standard HO-3 policy; national average premium for $420k home with $250k+ dwelling coverage
notes: PV at 3% real: $2,400 x 19.6004 = $47,041. Premiums vary widely by location, especially coastal/disaster-prone areas.

assumptions: ~10,500 kWh/yr at ~$0.114/kWh national average; includes cooling, lighting, appliances
locator: Table 5.6.a - Average Retail Price of Electricity
quote_excerpt: Average residential electricity rate and consumption
notes: PV at 3% real: $1,200 x 19.6004 = $23,520. Assumes constant real utility costs.

assumptions: Gas furnace heating + gas water heater + gas range; ~600 therms/yr at ~$1.50/therm
quote_excerpt: Average residential natural gas price and consumption

assumptions: Municipal water and sewer service for 3BR/2.5BA household (~60,000 gal/yr)
quote_excerpt: Average combined water and sewer bill for residential customers

assumptions: Sum of P025 + P026 + P027. Energy $2,100 + water $500 = $2,600/yr
Industry rule of thumb: 1% of home value annually for routine maintenance (landscaping, cleaning, minor repairs, pest control, seasonal maintenance)
quote_excerpt: Annual maintenance typically runs 1-2% of home value
notes: PV at 3% real: $4,200 x 19.6004 = $82,322. Excludes major capital replacements counted separately.

assumptions: Asphalt shingle roof replacement at year 25 (25-30 year shingle lifespan); ~30 squares at ~$400/square installed
quote_excerpt: Asphalt shingle roof replacement cost for typical home

assumptions: Central AC + gas furnace replacement at year 17 (15-20 year typical lifespan). 3-ton system.
Standard 50-gal gas water heater, ~12 year lifespan. Two replacements during 30-year ownership.
quote_excerpt: Standard tank water heater installed cost
notes: Nominal total: $3,600. PV at 3%: $2,148.

assumptions: Major kitchen appliance suite replacement (refrigerator, range, dishwasher, microwave) at year 13. Lifespan 10-15 years.
quote_excerpt: Average kitchen appliance suite replacement cost

assumptions: Full exterior repaint every 10-12 years. ~2,500 ft2 exterior surface area, $1.60/ft2 paint + labor.
locator: Exterior Painting Cost per Square Foot
quote_excerpt: Average cost to paint house exterior
notes: Nominal total: $8,000. PV at 3%: $4,773.

assumptions: Partial flooring replacement (~1,000 ft2 of high-traffic areas) at year 15. LVP or carpet at $6/ft2 installed.
Sum of P030-P035. Major capital items replaced during 30-year ownership period.
quote_excerpt: Major home system and component replacement costs

assumptions: Minimal HOA for SF subdivision covering common area maintenance, neighborhood upkeep. Many SF homes have no HOA; this represents a low HOA community.
locator: Average HOA Fees by Housing Type
notes: PV at 3% real: $600 x 19.6004 = $11,760. Many SF homes have $0 HOA; $600/yr is conservative low-end for HOA communities.

assumptions: 30-year total cost of ownership. All values in constant 2024 USD. Excludes home equity/appreciation (this is cost, not net wealth).
source_title: Composite calculation from all provenance entries
notes: Represents total undiscounted cash outflows over 30-year ownership. Does not include opportunity cost of down payment or home equity buildup.

assumptions: All future cash flows discounted at 3% real. Year-0 costs undiscounted. Annual costs use annuity factor 19.6004. Lump-sum replacements discounted individually.
source_title: Composite calculation from all provenance entries
notes: Present value of all ownership costs at 3% real discount rate.

formula: Census median new SF home price, adjusted to 2024
assumptions: Median new single-family home sale price from Census/HUD New Residential Sales, ~$420k in 2023-2024
locator: Median Sales Price of New Houses Sold
quote_excerpt: Median sales price of new single-family houses sold
notes: Reference value used to derive lot cost, property tax base, maintenance percentage, and financing amounts. Not a direct Sankey flow.

formula: 10% of $300,000 total development cost per unit
assumptions: NAHB MF cost survey: land 10-12% of total dev cost. Garden-style suburban = lower end.
source_title: Cost of Constructing a Multifamily Building
notes: Enormous regional variation. $15k-$60k/unit range nationally.

formula: 8-10% of dev cost allocated to site work, grading, paving, utilities
assumptions: NAHB MF data: site work 8-10%. Garden-style with surface parking.
source_title: Cost of Constructing a Multifamily Building
quote_excerpt: Site work including grading, utilities, paving
notes: Surface parking reduces site cost vs structured parking.

assumptions: NAHB/NMHC: A/E fees 5-8% of hard costs for garden-style MF.
quote_excerpt: Architecture and engineering 5-8% of hard costs
notes: Includes structural, MEP, civil, landscape architecture.

assumptions: National average permit fee for 100-unit MF project
source_title: Government Regulation in the Price of a New Home
quote_excerpt: Building permit fees $800-$1,500 per MF unit
notes: Highly variable by jurisdiction; includes plan review fees.

formula: National median impact fees for MF unit
assumptions: NAHB: $3k-$15k range nationally; $5k median for garden-style outside CA
source_title: Cost of Regulations to Multifamily Housing Development
notes: $5k is below NMHC avg because we model national typical, not high-cost markets.

formula: Legal entitlement + Phase I + geotech / 100 units
assumptions: Legal: $5k-$15k project; Phase I: $3k-$6k; geotech: $5k-$10k
quote_excerpt: Legal, environmental, and other soft costs
notes: Assumes by-right project; discretionary review would increase substantially.

assumptions: Water, sewer, electric, gas service connections for 100-unit project
source_title: Government Regulation in the Price of a New Home
notes: Varies widely; some jurisdictions require main extensions.

assumptions: NAHB/RSMeans: concrete/foundation 6-9% of hard costs for MF
notes: Slab-on-grade; 3-story wood-frame.

assumptions: Wood framing is largest single cost division at 12-18% of hard costs
quote_excerpt: Wood, plastics, composites 12-18% of hard costs
notes: Includes sheathing, trusses, hardware.

formula: ~11% of hard costs: siding, windows, doors, roofing, waterproofing
assumptions: Thermal/moisture 5-8% + doors/windows 4-6%
notes: Includes waterproofing, insulation, windows, roofing.

formula: ~21% of hard costs: plumbing 7% + HVAC 8% + electrical 10% + fire 3%
assumptions: Combined MEP + fire suppression divisions
notes: Fire sprinkler required for 3-story; adds ~$3/ft2.

formula: ~24% of hard costs: drywall, paint, flooring, cabinets, counters, fixtures
assumptions: Finishes 10-14% + specialties 1-2% + equipment/appliances 3-5%
notes: Mid-market finish level for garden-style apartments.

formula: ~9% of hard costs allocated to common areas
assumptions: Lobbies, corridors, leasing office, fitness center, package room
source_title: Cost of Constructing a Multifamily Building
notes: Garden-style has lower common area costs than mid-rise.

assumptions: Surface parking at $5k-$10k/space; 1.2-1.5 spaces/unit; landscape $2k-$5k/unit
source_title: Cost of Constructing a Multifamily Building
notes: Surface parking only; structured would be 3-5x more.

assumptions: Developer overhead 3-5% of total dev cost
source_title: Cost of Constructing a Multifamily Building
notes: Internal staff, office, due diligence, insurance.

assumptions: Market-rate developer profit 8-15%
source_title: Cost of Constructing a Multifamily Building
notes: Varies by market cycle and project risk.

assumptions: 18-month construction period, 65% average outstanding balance, SOFR+300bps
notes: Rate-sensitive; 2024 SOFR ~5.3%, total ~8%.

formula: 1 point on $210,000 permanent loan + closing costs
assumptions: Fannie/Freddie permanent financing; ~1% origination
notes: Agency financing is most common for stabilized garden-style.

formula: $210,000 loan at 6.5% × 30yr = $477,600 total payments - $210,000 principal = $267,600 interest
assumptions: 70% LTV on $300k; 6.5% fixed 30yr; agency permanent financing
notes: PV at 3% real: $175,049. Rate-sensitive assumption.

assumptions: Third-party mgmt fee ~4% of EGI for 100+ unit property
quote_excerpt: Management fee 3-5% of effective gross income
notes: Well-established industry benchmark.

assumptions: Leasing staff + maintenance staff allocated per unit
notes: Largest single operating expense line item.

assumptions: ~0.67% effective rate on $300k/unit assessed value; MF often assessed lower
notes: MF effective rate often lower than SF due to assessment methods.

assumptions: National average MF insurance per unit; excludes flood
notes: Rising rapidly; Sun Belt/coastal can be 2-3x this level.

assumptions: Common area electric + owner-paid water/sewer + gas
notes: RUBS recovery can offset 60-85% of owner-paid utilities.

assumptions: Routine maintenance and minor repairs per unit
notes: Includes plumbing, electrical, appliance repairs, common area maintenance.

assumptions: ~50% annual turnover × $1,600 make-ready cost per turn
notes: Paint, carpet, cleaning, minor repairs per turnover.

assumptions: Advertising, accounting, legal, office supplies
notes: Includes online listing fees, accounting, legal.

assumptions: Landscaping, pest control, elevator service, fire system maintenance
notes: No elevator in 3-story garden-style; mainly landscaping and pest.

assumptions: Commercial roof 25-30yr life; $8-$14/ft2
notes: Garden-style may have pitched shingle roofs.

assumptions: In-unit PTAC or split system; 15-20yr life
notes: Garden-style typically has individual units.

assumptions: Individual tank water heaters; 10-15yr life
notes: Tank style; tankless would last 20+ years.

assumptions: Range, dishwasher, refrigerator, disposal package
notes: Builder-grade appliances; shorter life than residential.

assumptions: LVP/vinyl plank replacement; higher turnover in rental
notes: Rental units have higher wear; LVP increasingly common.

assumptions: Exterior repaint/touch-up cycle 7-12 years
notes: Building-wide repaint; per-unit allocation.

assumptions: Surface lot reseal every 7yr; overlay at 20yr
notes: Timing variable; condition-dependent.

assumptions: Signage, amenity equipment, mailboxes, etc.
notes: Estimated; actual driven by reserve study.
